content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

January 15, 1991

Anne M. Russell
DIEHL & RODEWALD
Attorneys at Law
1011 Pacific Street
P.O. Box 1207
San Luis Obispo, CA  93406

                                    Re: Your Request for Advice
                                        Our File No. I-90-690

Dear Ms. Russell:

     This is in response to your August 7, 1990 letter requesting
advice concerning the conflict of interest provisions of the
Political Reform Act ("Act").¹ Your request is one for general
assistance. Accordingly, we are treating it as a request for
informal assistance.²

     You have asked the Fair Political Practices Commission (the
"Commission") to review the conflict of interest code for the
Santa Maria Public Airport District ("district") to determine
whether your code needs to be amended and to provide you with any
suggestions for improvement.

     As you are aware, the Commission is not the code reviewing
body for the district. Pursuant to Government Code Section 82011,
the Commission has authority over the codes of multi-county
jurisdictional agencies and state agencies only. The following
information is offered to assist you with the process of amending
your code and is based solely on the documentation submitted with
your request. We are unable to provide assistance with the
designation or appropriate assignment of disclosure categories as
no job duty statements or organizational charts were submitted.

_______________
1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section
18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

Anne M. Russell
Page 2


     In conducting a cursory review of your code, there are some
amendments we suggest be made to update the code to conform to
current requirements.

     1.  Government Code Section 81008 provides that statements of
economic interests are public records and should be available for
public inspection.  In an effort to assist you in making this
amendment, I have provided a sample of how your incorporation page
should appear.  Because the incorporation page of the code
incorporates 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18730 and
any amendments thereto into your code, it is only necessary to
have Exhibit "A" and Exhibit "B" attached to the incorporation
page.  Therefore, pages 403-405 should be removed.

     2.  Regulation 18730 was amended to conform it to the
provisions of Section 87302, as amended, effective January 1,
1990.  Prior to this amendment, income was not an item required to
be reported on either the assuming office statement or the initial
statement of economic interests.  I have enclosed the current
version of Regulation 18730 for your reference.

     Your conflict of interest code does not require a person
filing an assuming office statement, or filing an initial
statement pursuant to a newly adopted or amended conflict of
interest code, to disclose income received in the previous twelve
months.  Regulation 18730 as it now reads requires (1) persons
assuming office to disclose, in their statement of economic
interests, income received during the 12 months prior to the
filing of the statement, and (2) persons filing an initial
statement, under a newly-adopted or amended conflict of interest
code, to disclose income received during the 12 months prior to
the effective date of the code.

     3.  Your code at present does not provide for the disclosure
of financial interests by consultants for the agency.  The
definition of designated employee includes consultants.  (Section
82019.)  A consultant is any natural person who provides under
contract, information, advice, recommendation or counsel to any
agency.  (Regulation 18700, copy enclosed.)

     The Commission realizes that not all consultants participate
in the making of decisions on behalf of public agencies.  Rather
than amend your code each time you retain a consultant that is in
a decision-making capacity, you may use this specialized
disclosure category that provides that the disclosure required of
consultants will be determined on a case-by-case basis by the
executive officer of the agency.  The executive officer would make
a determination as to what disclosure, if any, is required by any
particular consultant.  A copy of this consultant disclosure
category language is attached, and should be incorporated into
your code.

Anne M. Russell
Page 3


     The position "Consultant*" would be the last position
designated in the appendix. The asterisk would bring the reader
down to the consultant disclosure category at the bottom of that
page.

     4.  Government Code Section 87302 has been amended to require
that disclosure categories specifically include the disclosure of
business positions. Your disclosure categories need to be amended
at each place where investments must be disclosed to now read
"Investments and business positions in...." For example, category
1 should read "Investments and business positions in any business
entity which...."

     5.  Appendix "B" of your code includes several limitations in
the subparagraphs under the "Note" categories as well as
categories 5 and 6. If these limitations were amended
statutorily, the district would be required to amend its code to
bring it current with the new law. With the exception of
subparagraph (1) under category 5 on page 409, we would suggest
deleting these notes in each disclosure category. This
information is also set out in the disclosure forms.

     6.  We have a problem with the disclosure required in
category 6. That category requires the disclosure of real
property and investments held by a business entity only if the
filer has a 50% or greater interest in the business entity. The
Act provides that such interests would be disclosed if the filer
has a 10% or greater interest in the business entity and the
property or investment is of the type covered by the disclosure
categories of the code. (Section 82034) This general disclosure
is already covered under the disclosure of investments and
interests in real property. Category 6 should be deleted in its
entirety.

     In an effort to assist you in making these amendments, I have
provided a sample of how your conflict of interest code should
appear. The items discussed above have been incorporated in this
sample.

     7.  We would recommend that you perform a thorough review of
the district to ensure that it accurately reflects all persons in
a decision-making capacity and that the disclosure and
disqualification provisions of the Act have been properly
assessed.

Anne M. Russell
Page 4

If you have any questions, please feel free to contact me at
(916) 322-5901.

Very truly yours,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

[signature]
By: Lynda Doherty
Staff Services Analyst
Legal Division

:ld

Enclosures
